summary: The cradle of Western democracy and philosophy, a NATO and EU member that endured a devastating debt crisis (2010-2018) and remains strategically vital as a Mediterranean gateway between Europe, the Middle East, and North Africa.

history.detail: Athenian democracy, philosophy (Socrates, Plato, Aristotle), mathematics foundation of Western thought.
Conquers Persian Empire; Hellenistic culture spreads to India.
Ottomans take Constantinople; Greece under Ottoman rule until 1821.
Successful revolt; Kingdom of Greece established 1832.
Greece refuses Italian ultimatum; Axis occupation 1941-44; massive famine.
EU membership; structural funds drive growth.
€300B debt; three EU/IMF bailouts; decade of austerity.

resources.oil_gas: Potential offshore reserves disputed with Turkey

follow_the_money.detail: China's COSCO owns 67% of Piraeus; Greece is China's EU maritime entry point
Greeks own ~20% of global merchant fleet; world's largest shipping nation by tonnage

theories_disputed.detail: Declassified documents show US knowledge; direct CIA orchestration not conclusively proven.

connections.detail: Aegean disputes, Cyprus, EEZ gas exploration conflict
COSCO Piraeus investment; Belt and Road entry point
